jdk: 8 (Temurin) / Maven 3.9, Spring Boot 2.3.0, multi-module (jws-signature, diffie-hellman-services, webservice, kms)
partial_detail: fixed 2 of 5 (calendar day-field + trim normalization); 14/17 pass, the other 3 f2p (token index, UUID quantifier, hour format) still fail -> reward 0
litmus_result: PASS — instruction names no file/method/line/edit/count; highlights only 2 of 5 symptoms; fixing those 2 still fails >=3 hidden f2p
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN/EMPTY (all shipped JUnit 4 / PowerMock test sources are deleted at plant; they are never run by the surefire junit-platform provider anyway — junit-vintage is excluded — and none exercise the planted helpers; no src/test/*.java is visible to the agent)
offline_verified: all ladder runs executed with --network none; mvn -o -pl webservice test needs no network (siblings installed to local repo + surefire junit-platform provider warmed at repo-image build time); container has no DNS
env_note: No task-specific env or secrets required. Gold tests are pure-input JUnit 5 on static utils and a bare-constructed stateless parser (no Spring context, no DB). The targeted helpers are deterministic and offline.

defects.derivability_evidence: On-line pin: the argument is named `days` and the immediately following line is c.add(Calendar.MONTH, month); the field for `days` must be the day field (Calendar.DATE).
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: No shipped test exercises this static util; the day-boundary input is injected only at grade time.

defects.wrong_output: "@customer123" (the user part) instead of "@finvu" (the AA handle)
defects.derivability_evidence: Pinned by the `"@" +` re-prepend, the guard `if(customerIdSplit.size() > 1)` directly above (index 1 must exist and be the target), and the doc 'return the AA ID' — for a user@aa-handle VUA the handle is the token after '@' (index 1).
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Only a customerId containing '@' with a non-empty handle reaches the split; supplied only by the gold test. Method is pure (touches no injected field).

defects.wrong_output: false (valid v4 UUID rejected) instead of true
defects.derivability_evidence: Pinned by the canonical UUID layout spelled out in the same literal: {8}-{4}-{4}-{4}-{12}; the final group of a UUID is 12 hex digits. The preceding groups anchor the whole pattern.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: A full 36-char valid UUID is needed to distinguish {11} from {12}; malformed / wrong-version ids (the p2p cases) reject under both.

defects.wrong_output: false (a whitespace-only string is treated as non-empty) instead of true
defects.derivability_evidence: Pinned by the sibling clause `val.equals("null")` in the same return expression: the emptiness notion is deliberately lenient (even the literal string 'null' counts), so a blank-after-trim string must count too; the .trim() is what enforces it.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Only a whitespace-only (not empty, not null, not 'null') string distinguishes the two; the empty-string and literal-'null' p2p cases are invariant.

defects.edit: in forPattern(...): the hour token 'T'HH:mm  ->  'T'hh:mm (24-hour to 12-hour)
defects.derivability_evidence: Pinned by the 'Z' UTC ISO-8601 literal (24-hour by definition) and by every other date pattern in the repo using HH; these stamps are re-parsed downstream via DateTime.parse, which a 12-hour render breaks. Only afternoon times (hour > 12) reveal it.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Ordinary in-range times before noon render identically; the afternoon input is injected only at grade time.

fairness_audit: Gold tests drive pure static helpers and one bare `new ConsentInitServiceImpl()` whose called method (validateCustomerId) reads only its String argument — no @Autowired collaborator is touched, so there are no mocks, no Spring context, no reflection, and no private-name or module coupling; nothing encodes the oracle's implementation. Each of the five oracle values is derivable from the code itself (adjacent days/month .add pairing, a size()>1 guard + '@' re-prepend, the canonical 8-4-4-4-12 UUID literal, a lenient sibling emptiness clause, a UTC ISO-8601 literal plus repo-wide HH), not from convention, and an alternative-implementation audit holds (any equivalent boundary correction passes). Cross-defect interference is contained: defects 1 and 5 co-locate on getAddedDate's output path, so the day-field f2p asserts only the date prefix and is unaffected by the hour defect; the NULL run shows exactly 5 failures and 0 errors, and each f2p fails solely on its own asserted behaviour. The instruction is a symptom-level report with two highlighted examples and names no file, method, line, edit direction, trigger value, or defect count; an agent fixing only the two highlighted symptoms still fails at least three hidden f2p.
resistance_assessment: Targeting Opus 1-4/10. A solve requires finding all five single-token slips spread across four files in a 264-file, multi-module Spring codebase, with no failing local test to point the way (the visible suite is empty/green) and only two of five symptoms surfaced in the instruction. Two are EASY (a days-vs-months Calendar field pinned by the adjacent argument; a dropped whitespace-trim pinned by its lenient sibling clause). Three are MEDIUM, pinned by cross-line / domain reasoning: the VUA handle index depends on the '@' re-prepend and size guard; the UUID quantifier depends on knowing the canonical 8-4-4-4-12 layout; the hour format depends on recognising an ISO-8601 UTC stamp must be 24-hour and only misbehaves past noon. Five distinct edit shapes (calendar field, token index, regex quantifier, trim normalization, hour token) defeat grep-for-the-twin; a literal-fix agent that patches only the two reported symptoms misses >=3 hidden tests.
